title: Fix nullable Array.shift class return lowering
description: codex-hxrust HXCX-4.7 exposed a generic haxe.rust lowering bug: a Haxe method returning Null<ClassType> from Array<ClassType>.shift() generated a Rust function returning HxRef<ClassType> while the expression lowered to Option<HxRef<ClassType>>, causing E0308. Add a product-neutral regression fixture and lower nullable Array.shift/optional class returns as Option/Null consistently.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal Haxe fixture with Array<Class>.shift() returned as Null<Class> fails before the fix and passes after; generated Rust type signature and expression agree on Option/Haxe null representation; no app-specific or Codex-specific code is added; portable generated cargo gate passes.

title: Fix static final class field access path lowering
description: codex-hxrust HXCX-4.23 exposed that reading a same-class static final String from a harness method can generate Rust that calls crate::<class_module>::__hx_static_get_* even when that module is not emitted at crate root, causing E0433. Reduce to a product-neutral fixture and fix static final/static var access path lowering.
acceptance_criteria: Minimal haxe.rust fixture reads a same-class static final String from another static method; generated Rust compiles without missing crate module paths; existing static var helper snapshots remain green; codex-hxrust can remove its local helper-function workaround if desired.

title: Eliminate remaining haxe_crypto_smoke Rust lint warnings via codegen (postfix local unop + path-aware mut + unused local underscore)
notes: Root fixes in RustCompiler: postfix local ++/-- value-vs-stmt lowering split, unread immutable locals lowered to underscore bindings, and path-sensitive mutability for uninitialized locals to avoid false positive let-mut declarations.

title: Fix optional primitive constructor default lowering to Rust
description: Generated Rust can lower an optional primitive constructor parameter with a default as Option<T> and then assign it directly into a non-optional class field. A downstream consumer hit this with a Haxe constructor parameter shaped like ?useResponsesLite:Bool = false assigned into a Bool field; generated Rust assigned Option<bool> into bool. This should be fixed generically for optional primitive default parameters, not by adding consumer-specific behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Portable and metal generated Rust compile for a minimal fixture where a constructor has optional primitive defaults (?flag:Bool = false, ?count:Int = 0, ?name:String = "") assigned into non-optional fields; generated code unwraps/defaults before assignment instead of assigning Option<T>; regression fixture is generic and contains no downstream project names or paths

title: Spike: align reflaxe.rust to family portable|metal contract after haxe.go canonicalization
description:
## Why
Cross-compiler investigation indicates the family should standardize on `portable|metal` as profile contracts, with profiles as presets over orthogonal axes (strictness, runtime policy, interop policy), while preserving target-specific internals.

## Inputs
- Canonical direction from `../haxe.go/docs/portable-canonical-contract.md`
- Go phase roadmap: `../haxe.go/docs/phase2-roadmap.md`
- Go selective runtime plan: `../haxe.go/docs/hxrt-selective-runtime.md`
- Rust implementation references:
  - `src/reflaxe/rust/ProfileResolver.hx`
  - `src/reflaxe/rust/analyze/HxrtFeatureAnalyzer.hx`
  - `src/reflaxe/rust/emit/ProjectEmitter.hx`
  - `src/reflaxe/rust/passes/NoHxrtPass.hx`
  - `docs/profiles.md`, `docs/metal-profile.md`

## Spike Scope
1. Validate where Rust already matches the family model and where terminology/contracts drift.
2. Define a family-aligned artifact contract for Rust:
   - profile contract report
   - runtime plan report (features selected + reasons)
3. Propose minimal changes needed for explicit family parity without regressing Rust-specific strengths (feature slicing, no-hxrt, fallback).

## Deliverables
- `docs/spikes/family-portable-metal-alignment.md` (or equivalent) with:
  - current state
  - gap matrix
  - migration tasks
  - risk notes
- Beads follow-up tasks created from that matrix (all low priority unless blocking).

## Future Follow-up Trigger
After `haxe.go` lands selective runtime slicing + metal fallback + runtime-plan reporting, run a new GPT-5.2 Pro cross-repo spike comparing latest Go/Rust/Elixir/hxhx and refresh family guidance.

title: v1.0: String nullability + representation
description:
Decide and implement the v1.0 representation for Haxe String nullability.

Problem:
- Haxe String is nullable by default, but Rust String cannot represent null.
- We already have hxrt::string::HxString(Option<String>) and Dynamic boxing supports it, but typed String currently maps to Rust String in many places.

Work:
- Choose v1.0 rule for Portable + Idiomatic profiles (recommendation: map Haxe String to hxrt::string::HxString, with ergonomic helpers so most code works with &str/String internally).
- Update codegen type mapping, literals, concatenation, Std.string/trace/println, and StringTools to handle null correctly.
- Ensure Rusty profile can opt into non-null Rust String if desired (explicit flag, documented).

Acceptance:
- Haxe code "var s:String = null; Sys.println(s);" prints "null" and does not panic.
- String concatenation with null matches Haxe semantics.
- Snapshots updated; examples still pass.
notes: 2026-02-12 follow-up: snapshot goldens regenerated for impacted cases after nullable-string bridges and string-mode policy changes. Updated with --update and passing build/diff checks: string_null_print_concat, string_concat, v1_smoke, sys_io, tier1_stdlib, sys_db_sqlite_smoke, sys_db_mysql_compile, sys_http_smoke. Prior focused compile/runtime checks still hold (null print/concat semantics, portable default nullable mode, rust_string_non_nullable override, conflicting define guard).

Equivalent Haxe works under interp; codexhx had to use value.substr(0, value.length) copies as a workaround. This should be fixed by clone insertion/reuse analysis for non-Copy locals used as expression results across later uses, without Codex-specific code.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal product-neutral Haxe regression reuses one String local as the then branch result for multiple conditional expressions and compiles to Rust without E0382 move-after-move; generated Rust inserts clones only where needed for non-Copy values; portable generated cargo gate passes; no Codex-specific code is added.

The consumer workaround was to materialize all fields with a default factory, but the compiler/runtime should preserve Haxe optional-field semantics generically.
acceptance_criteria: Add a minimal haxe.rust regression fixture for a typed anonymous structure with optional String/Bool fields omitted at construction and read later; generated Rust must not panic and must match Haxe semantics for null/default checks; fix lowering/runtime generically without Codex-specific code; document the behavior if needed.

acceptance_criteria: - Constructor field init avoids redundant clones\n- Snapshots updated\n- CI green

title: Constructor arg clone elision
description: When lifting constructor field assignments into the struct init, avoid `.clone()` for Copy args and for non-Copy args used only once in the constructor body.
acceptance_criteria: - Snapshots show fewer .clone() in new()\n- No move errors\n- CI green
notes: Constructor lifting now avoids `.clone()` for Copy args and for non-Copy ctor args used only once; reduces clone noise in generated new() (see tui_todo Task::new).

title: Make haxe.ds.List iterator metal-clean
description: Codexhx native SQLite metal boundary pulled in sys.db.ResultSet, whose results() signature pulls haxe.ds.List. haxe.ds.List.iterator currently emits raw ERaw via untyped __rust__, so strict metal rejects the otherwise typed boundary. Replace it with typed Array-backed iteration and add/refresh product-neutral snapshot evidence.
acceptance_criteria: haxe.ds.List iterator no longer uses raw Rust; list_minimal or equivalent snapshot passes; codexhx native SQLite persistence metal gate can compile without relying on Codex-specific compiler behavior.
close_reason: Fixed by moving haxe.ds.List iterator construction to a typed ListNative helper and refreshing list_minimal snapshot evidence.

title: Fix anonymous Int field read runtime downcast
description: codex-hxrust HXCX-4.122 exposed a generic haxe.rust runtime/lowering gap: Haxe interpreter accepted a typed anonymous structure carrying an Int field named functionNumber, but generated Rust compiled and then panicked at hxrt/src/anon.rs with 'anon field has wrong type: functionNumber' when reading the field. The consumer workaround was to replace the internal anonymous record with a small typed class; haxe.rust should preserve typed anonymous Int field reads without requiring that source rewrite.
acceptance_criteria: Add a minimal haxe.rust regression fixture for a typed anonymous structure with required Bool/String/enum-abstract/Int fields returned from helper functions and read later; generated Rust must not panic and must match Haxe interpreter semantics for the Int field; fix lowering/runtime generically without Codex-specific code; document interaction with existing optional anonymous-field issue haxe.rust-i8li if the fix overlaps.

title: v1.0: upstream stdlib sweep harness
description:
Add a deterministic harness that compiles (and preferably cargo-checks) a broad set of upstream Haxe std modules with -D rust_emit_upstream_std, so we can measure stdlib parity and catch regressions.

Scope:
- Create a new snapshot-style case or script that imports a curated list of modules (start with haxe.crypto/*, haxe.Json, haxe.Serializer/Unserializer, haxe.ds.*, haxe.io.*, haxe.zip.*, haxe.xml.*, etc.).
- Exclude compile-time-only packages (haxe.macro, haxe.display).
- Ensure it runs in CI without taking > a few minutes (curate list; avoid huge reflection paths).

Acceptance:
- New harness runs in CI and is stable (same stdout / same generated Rust passes cargo fmt/build).
- When the harness fails, it prints a short actionable error (which module/import triggered it).
notes: Implemented deterministic upstream stdlib sweep harness: added test/upstream_std_modules.txt + test/run-upstream-stdlib-sweep.sh (per-module compile with -D rust_emit_upstream_std + cargo fmt/check + actionable module/stage failures). Integrated into scripts/ci/harness.sh so it runs on every test:all/CI push.

title: Support haxe.io.Path lowering
description: codex-hxrust HXCX-5.1 exposed that importing haxe.io.Path and calling Path.directory generates references to crate::haxe_io_path::Path, but the generated crate does not define that module. Add stdlib lowering/runtime coverage for haxe.io.Path helpers at least Path.directory, or document unsupported status with a deterministic compiler diagnostic.
notes: Generic expected-failure repro added at test/repro/upstream_open_gaps/path_directory and covered by scripts/ci/check-upstream-open-gap-repros.sh. This is a contract fixture only; implementation remains open.
close_reason: Implemented generic haxe.io.Path.directory lowering via hxrt::path::directory, promoted path_directory to a passing snapshot with stdout coverage, refreshed hxrt snapshot runtime copies, and verified the snapshot, hxrt unit test, std_string_dynamic snapshot, and no-open-gap checker.

title: Support non-copy class field assign-op lowering
description: codex-hxrust HXCX-4.8 exposed a generic haxe.rust lowering gap: appending to a class field string with field += value produced 'Unsupported assignop field lvalue (non-copy) for Rust target'. Equivalent Haxe source using field = field + value compiles. Add a product-neutral regression and lower non-copy field assign-op by reading/cloning as needed, evaluating RHS safely, and writing the updated field without app-specific source contortions.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal Haxe fixture with a class String field updated via this.field += value fails before the fix and passes after; generated Rust preserves Haxe evaluation/order semantics for non-copy field assign-op; no Codex-specific code is added; portable generated cargo gate passes.

title: Language: instance method values (obj.method)

- Snapshot case covers method values + calling the resulting function

title: Language: compound assignment on instance fields

title: Roadmap: reflaxe.rust milestones (0–10)
description:
Tracks the milestone plan from `prd.md` (section 8).

Scope: Haxe 4.3.7 → Rust target built on Reflaxe with an AST-first pipeline (Builder → Transformer → Printer) and a Rust runtime crate (hxrt).

Primary outputs: haxelib + generated Cargo project (portable mode default; rust-native mode opt-in).
notes: Roadmap continuation: next scheduled milestone is haxe.rust-oo3.34 (Systems and environment proof-depth hardening). Concurrency depth is now materially improved; remaining explicit defers cluster around systems/environment proof depth and platform-sensitive claims.

title: Spike: scoped raw __rust__ authority metadata aligned with haxe.go low-level abstraction escape hatch
description: Design and implement a narrow metadata-based authority island for raw __rust__ injection in reflaxe.rust. Compare the current Rust strict/boundary enforcement and metal-island design against the local haxe.go implementation of @:goAllowRaw, then decide the Rust-appropriate metadata shape and enforcement boundaries. Carry over the rationale from haxe.go: raw target injection was restricted to prevent business-logic sprawl and preserve portable/profile enforcement, but user-owned low-level abstraction modules need a scoped escape hatch. Keep the design well documented with Why/What/How and make clear whether metal islands still reject raw injection.
acceptance_criteria: 1) The task compares local reflaxe.rust enforcement/architecture against local haxe.go scoped raw-injection authority design instead of blindly copying it. 2) It records the rationale from haxe.go for why raw injection was restricted by default and why a scoped authority island is the intended compromise for low-level abstraction modules. 3) It defines the Rust metadata name, enforcement boundaries, and documentation expectations before implementation. 4) It includes an explicit note to preserve or intentionally revise existing metal-island policy rather than accidentally weakening it.
close_reason: Implemented scoped raw __rust__ authority metadata, documented the policy, added strict positive and metal-island negative coverage, and validated with targeted checks plus a full harness pass

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Decision: add `@:rustAllowRaw` as a narrow strict-boundary authority island only. It can bypass `reflaxe_rust_strict` / `reflaxe_rust_strict_examples` for the tagged module, but it does not bypass `metal` or `@:haxeMetal`; raw fallback remains rejected there by metal-clean enforcement.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text:
Audit findings:
- bytes portable vs metal warning was partly a benchmark-contract problem. Raising bytes/json in-process sample counts from 20 to 60 removed the earlier json false positive and reduced the bytes gap from ~1.20x to ~1.13x.
- json is not the next warning target under the tightened protocol. The remaining json cost is still a real runtime/lowering hotspot (Dynamic/DynObject/serde_json boundary), but it is no longer the immediate perf-gate offender.
- int64 is not a near-native parity signal. Generated Rust still uses HxRef-backed haxe.Int64 objects and word-level operations, so the pure-Rust i64 comparison is a portability-cost tracker, not a metal parity target.
- live soft warnings after the audit rerun are now: hot_loop_inproc_overhead.metal.runtime and portable_vs_metal.bytesRuntimePortableVsMetal.
- targeted rerun evidence: bytes portable/metal medians stabilized around 2.12ms vs 1.87ms (60 runs), json portable/metal around 5.38ms vs 5.36ms (60 runs), int64 portable/metal around 445.5ms vs 440.7ms (20 runs).

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text:

- Focused 80-run rerun on the generated metal binary vs the pure Rust baseline measured ~1.7575ms vs ~1.7316ms median (ratio ~1.015), which is materially below the earlier soft warning signal.

title: Milestone 34 — Async stabilization + entrypoint contract
description:
Why
- `rust_async` is the largest remaining product caveat that still reads as preview rather than stable production surface.
- The compiler/runtime path is real and useful today, but the supported contract is still intentionally narrow (static-only async methods, no stable async entrypoint story).

What
- Turn async from a preview-shaped promise into an explicit stabilized contract, or prove that one narrow blocker-only follow-up remains.

How
- Freeze the actual async contract and preview-exit criteria.
- Close the receiver/entrypoint gaps that currently force preview language.
- Strengthen evidence, examples, and docs so async claims match real proof depth.
acceptance_criteria:
- A canonical async contract doc exists and classifies what is stable vs unsupported.
- Async receiver semantics are supported beyond static-only, or one explicit blocker is identified with narrow scope.
- A stable async entrypoint story exists and is covered by tests/examples.
- Docs/examples/evidence no longer rely on vague preview language if the contract is stable; otherwise the remaining blocker is explicit and bounded.
notes:
Strategic fit:
- Async is the remaining caveat that most directly limits the repo's "best way to write production Rust outside raw Rust" goal.
- Do not broaden portable/reflaxe.std scope here. Keep this milestone focused on Rust-first async stabilization and honest evidence.

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text:
Second-pass review for this thinking:xhigh milestone:

Decision review:
- We did not broaden the async contract beyond what the compiler/runtime evidence supports.
- The supported subset is now explicit and internally consistent: `metal` + `rust_async`, `Future<T>`, static/generated-class instance async methods, sync `main`, `Async.blockOn(...)` boundary.
- Unsupported edges remain explicit and intentionally enforced: async constructors, async `main`, portable async, `rust_no_hxrt` async.
- Public language now matches the implemented/evidenced subset instead of using vague preview wording.

Why closure is justified:
- receiver semantics are implemented and covered
- entry boundary is explicitly diagnosed, documented, and covered by dedicated fixtures/examples
- async evidence/docs/support-matrix surfaces now point to one canonical contract without overclaiming general async support

Residual non-blockers:
- this is still a Rust-first subset, not a blanket target-wide async claim
- future expansion should be a new milestone, not implicit scope creep on this one

title: Roadmap — v1.0: Rusty profile + idiomatic polish + lix
description:
Track the remaining work to reach a solid v1.0 of reflaxe.rust. Focus areas:

1) Rusty profile: opt-in mode that feels closer to Rust idioms and ecosystem types (owned containers, Option/Result, borrow-scoped APIs).
2) Idiomatic output polish: reduce noise in generated Rust (parens, unreachable arms, naming/warnings) without breaking Haxe semantics.
3) Tooling: lix-based dev workflow (pin Haxe) + CI alignment; GitHub-only releases for now.

title: Rusty profile — owned Rust types + borrow-scoped APIs
description: Implement the third profile (reflaxe_rust_profile=rusty) aimed at users who want Haxe syntax but closer-to-Rust idioms. Provide explicit rust.* APIs (Vec/Option/Result/HashMap) that map to std Rust types in generated code, plus borrow-scoped (closure-based) helpers so apps do not need raw __rust__.
acceptance_criteria:
- reflaxe_rust_profile=rusty has behavior differences vs idiomatic.
- std/rust exposes a minimal, documented rust.* API surface (Vec/Option/Result at least).
- Snapshot tests cover core rust.* usage patterns and compile+build cleanly.

title: Rusty profile: define API + ownership/borrow rules
description: Write a short design doc that defines what reflaxe_rust_profile=rusty means (vs portable/idiomatic), what guarantees it does/does not provide, and the initial rust.* API surface (Vec/Option/Result/HashMap). Include how borrow-scoped helpers work and what is considered "framework" vs "app" code (no raw __rust__ in apps).
acceptance_criteria:
- A doc exists in-repo describing the rusty profile and the initial rust.* APIs.
- The doc includes examples (Haxe code) and the expected generated Rust patterns.
- The doc defines what borrow-scoped helpers prevent/allow.

title: Rusty profile: rust.Vec<T> (std wrapper + compiler mapping)
description: Add a rust.Vec<T> type in std/rust that maps to Rust std::vec::Vec<T> in generated code under rusty profile. Provide core API: new(), fromArray(Array<T>), toArray(), len(), push(), pop(), get(), set(), iter(). Ensure no app-level __rust__ usage (wrap in std if needed).
acceptance_criteria:
- A new std/rust Vec API exists and compiles.
- Under reflaxe_rust_profile=rusty, rust.Vec<T> is emitted as Vec<T> (no hxrt container).
- Snapshots cover push/pop/index/iteration patterns.

title: Rusty profile: rust.Option<T> + rust.Result<T,E>
description: Add rust.Option<T> and rust.Result<T,E> types in std/rust that map to Rust Option<T> and Result<T,E> in generated code under rusty profile. Provide minimal ergonomic helpers (some/none, ok/err, isSome/isNone, unwrap/expect, map, and match helpers where feasible).
acceptance_criteria:
- rust.Option and rust.Result exist under std/rust.
- Under reflaxe_rust_profile=rusty they map to Option/Result in emitted Rust.
- Snapshots cover creation, branching (match/switch), and simple map usage.

title: Rusty profile: borrow-scoped helpers (withRef/withMut)
description: Add closure-scoped borrow helpers in std/rust (e.g. rust.Borrow.withRef / withMut) that let users safely borrow a value for a scope and prevent borrow leakage in generated Rust. First target: containers like rust.Vec and maps like rust.HashMap. Prefer zero-cost borrows in emitted Rust (use &T / &mut T), and keep the API Haxe-friendly.
acceptance_criteria:
- Borrow helpers exist and are documented with examples.
- Generated Rust uses &/&mut borrows with a tight scope.
- At least one snapshot demonstrates borrow helpers avoiding borrow-checker conflicts.

title: Rusty profile: snapshots + demo coverage
description: Add snapshot test cases that compile with reflaxe_rust_profile=rusty and exercise rust.Vec / rust.Option / rust.Result basics. Optionally add a small example (non-TUI) that uses the rusty APIs without __rust__.
acceptance_criteria:
- New snapshot cases exist and pass in CI.
- At least one case compiles with rusty profile and runs deterministically (stdout snapshot if applicable).

title: Rusty profile: rust.HashMap<K,V>
description: Add a rust.HashMap<K,V> type (std/rust) mapping to std::collections::HashMap<K,V> in generated code under rusty profile, with minimal API: new(), get(), insert(), remove(), containsKey(), keys()/values()/iter().
acceptance_criteria:
- rust.HashMap exists and maps to std::collections::HashMap in Rust output.
- Snapshots cover basic insert/get/remove patterns.

title: Idiomatic output — cleanup + naming strategy
description: Improve readability and reduce Rust warnings in generated output (especially under reflaxe_rust_profile=idiomatic and =rusty). Focus on expression parentheses, match exhaustiveness, mut inference, and a coherent naming strategy (snake_case without collisions or broken reflection).
acceptance_criteria:
- Expression printer uses precedence-aware formatting (no blanket parentheses).
- Match generation avoids unreachable wildcard arms when exhaustive.
- A documented plan exists for snake_case naming (with collision rules) and at least one part is implemented.

title: Idiomatic output: precedence-aware expression printing
description: Replace blanket parentheses around unary/binary/cast expressions with precedence-aware printing in RustASTPrinter. Goal: eliminate rustc warnings like unused_parens and improve readability without changing semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generated code no longer wraps every binary/unary/cast in parens.
- Snapshot suite updated and still passes (cargo fmt + build).
- At least the known warnings (unused_parens in while/index) are resolved.

title: Idiomatic output: remove unreachable match wildcard arms
description: When generating Rust match expressions, avoid emitting `_ => {}` if the match is already exhaustive (common for enums where we emit all variants). This removes rustc unreachable_patterns warnings and makes output cleaner.
acceptance_criteria:
- Generated enum matches do not include an unreachable wildcard arm.
- Snapshot(s) updated (tui_todo is a known case) and cargo build emits fewer warnings.

title: Idiomatic output: RustNaming module + collision rules
description: Create a centralized RustNaming helper (like reflaxe.elixir’s naming modules) for snake_case conversion, reserved keyword escaping, and collision avoidance. Document the rules (what happens if two identifiers map to the same snake_case). Start applying it to at least one category (e.g., generated module names and synthetic temporaries) as a first step.
acceptance_criteria:
- RustNaming exists with tests or snapshots proving deterministic behavior.
- Collision handling is defined and deterministic.
- At least one part of codegen uses RustNaming instead of ad-hoc string munging.

title: Idiomatic output: broaden mut inference + reduce warnings
description: Extend mutability inference (let vs let mut) to catch more mutation patterns (compound assigns, mutation through certain method calls, etc.) and reduce unused_mut warnings. Keep behavior strictly opt-in to idiomatic/rusty profiles.
acceptance_criteria:
- Idiomatic/rusty profiles produce fewer unused_mut warnings in snapshots/examples.
- No regressions in correctness (snapshots still pass).

title: Tooling — lix workflow + GitHub-only releases
description: Align dev and CI tooling with lix (like haxe.elixir.codex). Add npm+lix setup so the repo can be built/tested with a pinned Haxe, and keep releases GitHub-only for now (no haxelib publish step).

title: Spike: assess explicit auto profile vs portable|metal contracts
description:
Evaluate whether reflaxe.rust should keep explicit `portable|metal` profile contracts or introduce an explicit `auto` profile that infers runtime/dependency behavior while preserving semantic determinism.

Scope:
- Architectural spike only (no contract-changing implementation in this task).
- Produce evidence-backed recommendation for pre-1.0 and post-1.0 policy.
- Define non-goals and migration constraints if `auto` is pursued.

Core question:
- Can we reduce user-facing complexity without introducing semantic ambiguity, CI/snapshot instability, or hidden performance/behavior shifts?
acceptance_criteria: Produce a written recommendation with risks, migration policy, and clear boundaries between semantic contracts and additive inference.
notes:
Oracle decision context (source conversation):
- https://chatgpt.com/g/g-p-69965383a564819188b44c7003bcc173-processed-oracle-queries/c/699d17bd-de6c-8324-a993-828df9a30b00

Local repo context to incorporate:
- `src/reflaxe/rust/ProfileResolver.hx`: explicit `portable|metal` selector; alias removal.
- `src/reflaxe/rust/CompilerInit.hx`: profile-scoped define compatibility gates (`rust_async`, `rust_no_hxrt`, nullable string policy).
- `src/reflaxe/rust/analyze/ProfileContractAnalyzer.hx`: metal contract checks (reflection/dynamic-access/fallback defines/nullability).
- `src/reflaxe/rust/analyze/HxrtFeatureAnalyzer.hx` + `src/reflaxe/rust/emit/ProjectEmitter.hx`: additive selective hxrt inference + typed provenance.
- Report artifacts: `rust_profile_contract_report`, `rust_hxrt_plan_report` and deterministic CI guards.

Hypotheses to test:
1) Explicit semantic contracts (`portable|metal`) remain necessary long-term.
2) Inference should stay additive (runtime/dependency planning), never silently semantic.
3) An `auto` mode could exist only as explicit opt-in with deterministic report output and hard policy boundaries.

Research questions:
- How do official Haxe targets and other source-to-source compilers separate semantic mode contracts vs dependency/optimization inference?
- What failure modes appear when semantics are inferred from usage instead of declared?
- Which invariants must hold for CI/snapshot/perf determinism if an `auto` mode exists?

Deliverables:
- Decision memo with recommendation (`KEEP_TWO_PROFILES` or explicit `AUTO` design constraints).
- Risk register (top 5) + mitigations.
- Migration/deprecation matrix (pre-1.0 and post-1.0).
- Concrete bead plan (implementation tasks) only if recommendation includes new explicit `auto` profile.

Acceptance criteria:
- Recommendation is evidence-backed and references both local compiler constraints and external prior-art patterns.
- Explicitly states which behaviors must remain contract-declared vs which may be inferred.
- Includes clear “do not do” boundaries (no silent semantic flips, no hidden profile shifts).

title: Family hardening: fail fast on sibling target override collisions

This repo relies heavily on `std/**/*.cross.hx` and does not currently have early `src/haxe/*.cross.hx` ownership, but it still overlaps with sibling targets on modules such as `haxe.Exception`, `StringTools`, `Date`, `Lambda`, and `Sys`. Canonical family references live in sibling `../haxe.ocaml/docs/`:
- `../haxe.ocaml/docs/02-user-guide/CROSS_AND_STAGED_STDLIB_GUIDE.md`
- `../haxe.ocaml/docs/00-project/REFLAXE_FAMILY_CROSS_OVERRIDE_AUDIT.md`
- `../haxe.ocaml/docs/00-project/REFLAXE_FAMILY_CROSS_OVERRIDE_MATRIX.md`
Use those docs plus `docs/cross-overrides-and-hardening.md` here as the implementation brief.

title: Compiler: resolve pos file paths (avoid misclassifying upstream std as user)
description:
Fix path classification for emission. When deciding if a type should be emitted, resolve pos.file via Context.resolvePath (when relative) before comparing against CWD or std roots. This avoids accidentally treating upstream Haxe std files (which may appear as relative paths like haxe/IMap.hx) as user project files.

Acceptance:
- isUserProjectFile and isFrameworkStdFile operate on resolved absolute paths when possible.
- Snapshots still pass once upstream-emit rules are handled.

title: Avoid invalid PartialEq derive for enum payloads with non-PartialEq refs
description: Codexhx HXCX-4.15 introduced a typed Haxe enum carrying class request payloads. haxe.rust generated a Rust enum with #[derive(PartialEq)], but the variants contain HxRef<class> payloads and HxRef does not implement PartialEq, causing E0369. Fix enum derive/lowering generically so Haxe enums with non-PartialEq payloads still compile, either by omitting PartialEq where invalid or by providing a sound runtime equality strategy.
acceptance_criteria: A product-neutral snapshot covers an enum with class/reference payloads; generated Rust builds; codexhx native state adapter gate advances without Codex-specific compiler behavior.
close_reason: Fixed by implementing HxRef<T> PartialEq/Eq as Haxe reference identity and adding enum_ref_payload snapshot coverage.

title: Fix class field default constructor lowering with anonymous-field constructors
description: codex-hxrust HXCX-4.124 exposed a generated Rust panic when a class has a non-null class-typed field whose default struct initializer calls that field class constructor with Default::default() for its anonymous fields object. The generated call can pass a null HxRef<Anon> into the constructor, and the constructor immediately reads fields.borrow().get(...), panicking in hxrt::cell::HxRef::unwrap_cell. This is generic haxe.rust behavior and must not be fixed with codex-specific logic.
acceptance_criteria: Add a product-neutral Haxe repro with a class-typed field initialized through the generated Rust struct default path where the field class constructor takes an anonymous fields object; generated Rust must not call the field constructor with a null/default anonymous object, or it must emit a safe default object/nullable default as appropriate; cargo check/test/run should pass; document the lowering rule for class-typed field defaults; no codexhx-specific code.

title: Threading model: make Haxe values thread-safe
description: Decide and implement a correct threading strategy so sys.thread.Thread can execute Haxe code on OS threads without UB. Current runtime uses Rc<RefCell<T>> and non-Send closures, so true threading is blocked until we change the representation or enforce safe isolation.
acceptance_criteria:
- Document chosen model and its tradeoffs in docs/threading.md
- Implement minimal runtime/codegen support for sys.thread.Thread.create + message passing
- Add deterministic CI example that spawns threads and synchronizes via Lock/Mutex
- Ensure existing snapshots + examples remain green
